name: Tokyo

palette.name: Metropolitan Green
palette.rationale: The Tokyo metropolitan symbol is commonly presented in a vivid green associated with growth.

palette.name: Night Glass
palette.rationale: Dark glass and steel high-rises shape a useful neutral for Tokyo's evening skyline.

palette.name: Signal Red
palette.rationale: A bright urban accent captures crossings, signage, and high-density visual rhythm without official status.

palette.name: Sakura Mist
palette.rationale: Soft blossom pink adds a seasonal layer that is culturally legible but not year-round dominant.

palette.name: Transit Blue
palette.rationale: Cool electric blue supports Tokyo's rail-and-wayfinding identity as an editorial mobility cue.

palette.name: Temple Gold
palette.rationale: Muted gold references shrine and temple details within the larger contemporary city fabric.

palette.name: Concrete Pearl
palette.rationale: A light concrete neutral balances the palette for stations, streetscapes, and dense mixed-use blocks.

sources.title: Tokyo Metropolitan Government
sources.note: Used for official metropolitan identity context.

sources.title: Corporate Identity
sources.note: Used as transport-brand context for mobility-layer interpretation.

notes: Complete seed record with one official metropolitan identity color and editorial urban, seasonal, cultural, and mobility layers.
editorialSummary: Tokyo's complete starter palette pairs official metropolitan green with night glass, signal color, sakura softness, transit blue, temple gold, and concrete neutrals.
